element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn to manage basic shooting and movement patterns

First major combat encounter; teaches pattern recognition for boss behavior. NES version has Larios appear quickly after theme, making it harder to destroy core before combining (vs Japanese version where it takes longer)
Primary objective structures that must be destroyed to clear areas; introduces targeting strategy
Two-level weapon power system; players learn power progression mechanics
Secret optional structure requiring discovery and precision shooting; grants 1,000,000 point bonus. Reveals 'Mystery of the Goddess Continent' message (International versions only)
Culminating challenge before Infinity mode; defeating triggers ending sequence
Endless repetition of same level and boss without difficulty increase; tests endurance

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating tension through Greek-alphabet progression (Alpha to Omega); each area introduces sustained enemy pressure with distinct boss encounters as focal tension peaks. Relief provided by area-clear transitions and power-up acquisition. International version uses continuous main BGM with noise-channel percussion (more driving), while Japanese version uses sparse triangle-wave bass (more minimalist tension)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden Cleopatra target in Area Iota revealed by shooting specific area on right side of screen; optional discovery mechanic
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Cleopatra mask structure acts as hidden bonus trigger; requires active player experimentation to discover

difficulty_curve.early_game: Alpha area introduces basic enemies and Larios boss; moderate difficulty to establish gameplay fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Beta through Eta areas escalate enemy density and attack patterns; introduces power-up system progression
difficulty_curve.late_game: Theta through Omega areas feature sustained heavy enemy pressure and increasingly complex boss patterns; Cleopatra optional challenge appears in Iota
notable_spikes: Larios first boss encounter (especially NES version with faster appearance)
Omega final area before Infinity unlock
Cleopatra hidden target discovery (optional but rewarding)
Transition to Infinity mode after Omega (difficulty plateau rather than spike)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game structured as 15 sequential areas named after Greek alphabet letters (Alpha through Omega). Each area contains distinct enemy waves, a destructible area target structure, and culminates with Larios boss encounter. Creates clear visual/nomenclatural progression that signals advancement
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Alpha area (first) through Omega area (final standard level)

notable_level_patterns.description: Larios boss appears at end of each area with consistent theme and combat pattern. NES version features faster appearance timing than Japanese version, creating tighter pressure windows. Boss defeat triggers area-clear celebration and progression to next area

Optional Cleopatra target in Area Iota requires player to shoot specific screen location to reveal hidden structure. Defeating it grants 1,000,000 point bonus and triggers special message/theme (International versions). Rewards exploration and experimentation

After defeating Omega area target, player unlocks Infinity mode. Same level and boss repeat indefinitely without difficulty scaling. Shift from challenge-based progression to endurance-based meta-challenge
